 {"id":"bd-rhzu","title":"Test: gcc/g++/clang Remote Compilation","description":"## Purpose
Test that gcc, g++, and clang compilation commands are correctly offloaded to real workers.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_gcc_basic","phase":"setup","msg":"Preparing C compilation test","data":{"compiler":"gcc","fixture":"hello_c"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_gcc_basic","phase":"execute","msg":"Remote compilation","data":{"cmd":"gcc -o hello main.c","exit_code":0,"worker":"css","duration_ms":234}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_gcc_basic","phase":"verify","msg":"Binary verification","data":{"path":"hello","exists":true,"runs":true,"output":"Hello, World!"}}
```

## Test Cases

### GCC Compilation
1. Basic gcc:
   - Command: `gcc -o hello main.c`
   - Verify: hello binary produced locally
   - Verify: binary runs correctly
   - Log: command, exit code, binary verification

2. gcc with flags:
   - Command: `gcc -O2 -Wall -Werror -o hello main.c`
   - Verify: optimization applied, warnings treated as errors
   - Log: flags used, any warnings

3. gcc compilation only:
   - Command: `gcc -c main.c -o main.o`
   - Verify: object file produced
   - Log: object file size

### G++ Compilation
4. Basic g++:
   - Command: `g++ -o hello main.cpp`
   - Verify: binary produced
   - Log: C++ compilation success

5. g++ with C++ standard:
   - Command: `g++ -std=c++20 -o hello main.cpp`
   - Verify: C++20 features work
   - Log: standard version used

### Clang Compilation
6. Basic clang:
   - Command: `clang -o hello main.c`
   - Verify: binary produced
   - Log: clang version, output

7. clang++:
   - Command: `clang++ -o hello main.cpp`
   - Verify: C++ binary produced
   - Log: compilation success

### Error Handling
8. Compilation error:
   - Introduce syntax error
   - Verify: error message preserved
   - Verify: exit code non-zero
   - Log: error type, error message excerpt

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All compilers work on real workers
- [ ] Binaries produced and runnable locally
- [ ] Error messages preserved
- [ ] Exit codes correct
- [ ] ALL phases logged with structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:05.181678697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:29:15.075090611Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:29:15.074810433Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-v9pq (True E2E C/C++ Compiler Tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rhzu","depends_on_id":"bd-cg4i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:14.500574997Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhzu","depends_on_id":"bd-mnaa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:15.481434697Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhzu","depends_on_id":"bd-v9pq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:13.515184696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-rs7w","title":"Epic: Eliminate All Fleet Module Stubs for 1.0.0 Release","description":"# Epic: Eliminate All Fleet Module Stubs for 1.0.0 Release

## Background & Motivation

RCH 1.0.0 is being positioned as a production-ready release. During a deep code review, we discovered **three critical stubs** in the fleet module that return fake/hardcoded data instead of performing real operations. These stubs violate our core principle of NO TECH DEBT and make the following commands unreliable:

- `rch fleet preflight` - Returns hardcoded "all OK" without checking anything
- `rch fleet status` - Returns fake worker statuses without SSH
- `rch fleet rollback` - Simulates rollback with sleep() instead of restoring binaries

## Why This Matters

1. **User Trust**: Users running `rch fleet preflight` expect REAL checks. Reporting false positives erodes trust.
2. **Operational Safety**: Rollback is a critical recovery mechanism. A fake rollback that reports success while doing nothing is DANGEROUS.
3. **1.0.0 Quality Bar**: We cannot ship 1.0.0 with fake functionality. Every command must do what it claims.

## Scope

Replace all stub implementations with world-class, production-grade code that:
- Performs real SSH connections to workers
- Collects real metrics (disk space, tool versions, health)
- Handles errors gracefully with proper diagnostics
- Includes comprehensive tests (unit, integration, E2E)
- Follows RCH patterns (fail-open, structured errors, retry logic)

## Architecture Overview

All three functions need SSH access to workers. We should:
1. Reuse existing SSH infrastructure from `rch-common/src/ssh.rs`
2. Reuse retry logic from `rch/src/transfer.rs`
3. Follow the pattern established in `executor.rs` which was already fixed

## Files to Modify

- `rch/src/fleet/preflight.rs` - Main implementation
- `rch/src/fleet/rollback.rs` - Rollback implementation
- Possibly extract shared SSH utilities if patterns emerge

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All three functions perform real operations
- [ ] No hardcoded/fake return values
- [ ] Comprehensive error handling with RCH error codes
- [ ] Unit tests for all code paths
- [ ] E2E tests with real workers (or mock SSH)
- [ ] `cargo clippy` passes with no warnings
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Dependencies

This epic has no external blockers. It unblocks the 1.0.0 release.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:45:36.105920748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:07:18.377223342Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-rs7w.1","title":"Create Comprehensive E2E Test Scripts for Fleet Operations","description":"## Task
Create comprehensive end-to-end test scripts that verify all fleet operations work correctly
with real SSH connections and detailed logging.

## Background
User explicitly requested: "comprehensive unit tests and e2e test scripts with great, detailed 
logging so we can be sure that everything is working perfectly after implementation."

## E2E Test Script Requirements

### 1. Main Test Script: scripts/e2e_fleet_test.sh
\`\`\`bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# E2E Fleet Operations Test Suite
# Tests: preflight, fleet status, deployment, rollback
# 
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/e2e_fleet_test.sh              # Run with real workers from workers.toml
#   ./scripts/e2e_fleet_test.sh --localhost  # Run against localhost only
#   RCH_MOCK_SSH=1 ./scripts/e2e_fleet_test.sh  # Run with mock SSH

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "\${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "\$SCRIPT_DIR")"
LOG_FILE="\$PROJECT_ROOT/e2e_test_\$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"

# Logging functions with timestamps and levels
log_info()  { echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] [INFO]  \$*" | tee -a "\$LOG_FILE"; }
log_warn()  { echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] [WARN]  \$*" | tee -a "\$LOG_FILE" >&2; }
log_error() { echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] [ERROR] \$*" | tee -a "\$LOG_FILE" >&2; }
log_debug() { [[ "\${DEBUG:-}" == "1" ]] && echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] [DEBUG] \$*" | tee -a "\$LOG_FILE"; }

# Test result tracking
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0
TESTS_SKIPPED=0

run_test() {
    local name="\$1"
    shift
    log_info "=== Running test: \$name ==="
    if "\$@" 2>&1 | tee -a "\$LOG_FILE"; then
        log_info "✓ PASSED: \$name"
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        log_error "✗ FAILED: \$name"
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

# Test: Preflight checks
test_preflight() {
    log_info "Testing rch fleet preflight..."
    local output
    output=\$(RCH_JSON=1 rch fleet preflight --all 2>&1)
    
    # Verify JSON output structure
    echo "\$output" | jq -e '.data.results' > /dev/null || {
        log_error "Invalid preflight JSON output"
        return 1
    }
    
    # Check for actual SSH connectivity (not hardcoded true)
    local ssh_tested
    ssh_tested=\$(echo "\$output" | jq -r '.data.results[0].ssh_ok')
    log_debug "SSH connectivity result: \$ssh_tested"
    
    # Verify disk space is realistic (not hardcoded 10000)
    local disk_mb
    disk_mb=\$(echo "\$output" | jq -r '.data.results[0].disk_space_mb')
    if [[ "\$disk_mb" == "10000" ]]; then
        log_warn "Disk space appears to be hardcoded (10000 MB)"
    fi
    
    log_info "Preflight output saved to log"
    return 0
}

# Test: Fleet status
test_fleet_status() {
    log_info "Testing rch fleet status..."
    local output
    output=\$(RCH_JSON=1 rch fleet status 2>&1)
    
    echo "\$output" | jq -e '.data.workers' > /dev/null || {
        log_error "Invalid fleet status JSON output"
        return 1
    }
    
    # Verify workers were actually queried (not all hardcoded reachable=true)
    local reachable_count
    reachable_count=\$(echo "\$output" | jq '[.data.workers[] | select(.reachable == true)] | length')
    log_debug "Reachable workers: \$reachable_count"
    
    return 0
}

# Test: Rollback (dry run)
test_rollback_dry_run() {
    log_info "Testing rch fleet rollback --dry-run..."
    local output
    output=\$(RCH_JSON=1 rch fleet rollback --dry-run --all 2>&1) || true
    
    # Even if no backups, should get structured response
    echo "\$output" | jq -e '.success' > /dev/null || {
        log_error "Invalid rollback dry-run JSON output"
        return 1
    }
    
    return 0
}

# Main
main() {
    log_info "Starting E2E Fleet Test Suite"
    log_info "Log file: \$LOG_FILE"
    log_info "RCH version: \$(rch --version)"
    
    run_test "preflight" test_preflight
    run_test "fleet_status" test_fleet_status
    run_test "rollback_dry_run" test_rollback_dry_run
    
    log_info "=== Test Summary ==="
    log_info "Passed:  \$TESTS_PASSED"
    log_info "Failed:  \$TESTS_FAILED"
    log_info "Skipped: \$TESTS_SKIPPED"
    
    [[ \$TESTS_FAILED -eq 0 ]] || exit 1
}

main "\$@"
\`\`\`

### 2. Preflight-Specific Test: scripts/test_preflight.sh
\`\`\`bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Detailed preflight testing with verbose output

set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Preflight E2E Test ==="
echo "Testing SSH connectivity, disk space, tool availability..."

# Run with maximum verbosity
RUST_LOG=debug RCH_JSON=1 rch fleet preflight --all 2>&1 | tee preflight_debug.log

# Validate each check was actually performed
echo ""
echo "=== Validation ==="

# Extract results
results=\$(cat preflight_debug.log | grep -v "^20" | jq -r '.data.results[]')

# Check SSH was tested
if grep -q "ssh_ok.*true" preflight_debug.log; then
    echo "✓ SSH connectivity check performed"
else
    echo "✗ SSH connectivity may not have been tested"
fi

# Check disk space is realistic
if grep -qE '"disk_space_mb":[[:space:]]*[0-9]{4,}' preflight_debug.log && \
   ! grep -q '"disk_space_mb": 10000' preflight_debug.log; then
    echo "✓ Disk space appears to be real (not hardcoded)"
else
    echo "⚠ Disk space might be hardcoded (check value)"
fi

# Check tools were verified
if grep -q "which rsync" preflight_debug.log || grep -q "rsync_ok" preflight_debug.log; then
    echo "✓ rsync availability checked"
else
    echo "✗ rsync check may not have been performed"
fi

echo ""
echo "Full output saved to preflight_debug.log"
\`\`\`

### 3. Mock SSH Mode
For CI/testing without real workers:
\`\`\`bash
# In test scripts, detect and handle mock mode
if [[ "\${RCH_MOCK_SSH:-}" == "1" ]]; then
    log_info "Running in MOCK SSH mode"
    # Expected behavior: still return structured responses but with simulated data
fi
\`\`\`

## Logging Requirements

All test scripts must:
1. Use timestamped log entries: \`[2024-01-15 12:00:00] [INFO] message\`
2. Save full output to log file with unique timestamp
3. Show progress in real-time (tee to both stdout and log)
4. Include DEBUG level for verbose output when DEBUG=1
5. Show clear PASSED/FAILED indicators
6. Generate summary at end

## File Locations
- scripts/e2e_fleet_test.sh - Main test suite
- scripts/test_preflight.sh - Preflight-specific tests
- scripts/test_rollback.sh - Rollback-specific tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Main E2E script covers preflight, status, rollback
- [ ] Detailed timestamped logging to file
- [ ] Mock SSH mode for CI
- [ ] Clear pass/fail indicators
- [ ] Validates real behavior (not hardcoded values)
- [ ] Exit code reflects test results","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:59:35.515814231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:50.507050677Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rs7w.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2ch3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:59:46.608993087Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rs7w.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3029.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:59:44.349350303Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rs7w.1","depends_on_id":"bd-39j3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:59:48.859626439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rs7w.1","depends_on_id":"bd-rs7w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:59:35.515814231Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rs7w.1","depends_on_id":"bd-rs7w.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:50.506977219Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

# Test result tracking
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0
TESTS_SKIPPED=0

run_test() {
    local name="\$1"

    if "\$@" 2>&1 | tee -a "\$LOG_FILE"; then
        log_info "✓ PASSED: \$name"
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else

    local ssh_tested
    ssh_tested=\$(echo "\$output" | jq -r '.data.results[0].ssh_ok')
    log_debug "SSH connectivity result: \$ssh_tested"
    
    # Verify disk space is realistic (not hardcoded 10000)

    if [[ "\$disk_mb" == "10000" ]]; then
        log_warn "Disk space appears to be hardcoded (10000 MB)"
    fi
    
    log_info "Preflight output saved to log"
    return 0
}

# Test: Fleet status
test_fleet_status() {
    log_info "Testing rch fleet status..."
    local output
    output=\$(RCH_JSON=1 rch fleet status 2>&1)
    
    echo "\$output" | jq -e '.data.workers' > /dev/null || {
        log_error "Invalid fleet status JSON output"
        return 1
    }
    
    # Verify workers were actually queried (not all hardcoded reachable=true)
    local reachable_count
    reachable_count=\$(echo "\$output" | jq '[.data.workers[] | select(.reachable == true)] | length')
    log_debug "Reachable workers: \$reachable_count"
    
    return 0
}

# Test: Rollback (dry run)
test_rollback_dry_run() {
    log_info "Testing rch fleet rollback --dry-run..."

    output=\$(RCH_JSON=1 rch fleet rollback --dry-run --all 2>&1) || true

    echo "\$output" | jq -e '.success' > /dev/null || {
        log_error "Invalid rollback dry-run JSON output"
        return 1
    }
    
    return 0
}

# Main
main() {
    log_info "Starting E2E Fleet Test Suite"
    log_info "Log file: \$LOG_FILE"
    log_info "RCH version: \$(rch --version)"
    
    run_test "preflight" test_preflight
    run_test "fleet_status" test_fleet_status
    run_test "rollback_dry_run" test_rollback_dry_run
    
    log_info "=== Test Summary ==="
    log_info "Passed:  \$TESTS_PASSED"
    log_info "Failed:  \$TESTS_FAILED"
    log_info "Skipped: \$TESTS_SKIPPED"
    
    [[ \$TESTS_FAILED -eq 0 ]] || exit 1
}

main "\$@"

 {"id":"bd-rs7w.2","title":"Add Configurable Constants and User Feedback","description":"## Task
Add configurable constants and improve user feedback for fleet operations.

## Background
Currently, many values are hardcoded throughout the fleet module:
- Timeouts (SSH connect, command execution)
- Thresholds (min disk space, max load)
- Parallelism limits (concurrent SSH connections)
- Progress feedback (none currently)

Users should be able to configure these values and see progress during long operations.

## Configuration Requirements

### 1. Add Fleet Configuration Section to config.toml
\`\`\`toml
# ~/.config/rch/config.toml

[fleet]
# SSH timeouts
ssh_connect_timeout_secs = 10
ssh_command_timeout_secs = 30

# Health thresholds  
min_disk_space_mb = 500
max_load_average = 10.0

# Parallelism
max_concurrent_workers = 10

# Retry behavior
retry_count = 2
retry_delay_ms = 1000
\`\`\`

### 2. FleetConfig Struct
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(default)]
pub struct FleetConfig {
    pub ssh_connect_timeout: Duration,
    pub ssh_command_timeout: Duration,
    pub min_disk_space_mb: u64,
    pub max_load_average: f64,
    pub max_concurrent_workers: usize,
    pub retry_count: u32,
    pub retry_delay: Duration,
}

impl Default for FleetConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            ssh_connect_timeout: Duration::from_secs(10),
            ssh_command_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),
            min_disk_space_mb: 500,
            max_load_average: 10.0,
            max_concurrent_workers: 10,
            retry_count: 2,
            retry_delay: Duration::from_millis(1000),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Pass Config to Fleet Functions
\`\`\`rust
pub async fn run_preflight(
    worker: &WorkerConfig,
    ctx: &OutputContext,
    config: &FleetConfig,  // ADD THIS
) -> Result<PreflightResult>;

pub async fn get_fleet_status(
    workers: &[&WorkerConfig],
    ctx: &OutputContext,
    config: &FleetConfig,  // ADD THIS
) -> Result<Vec<WorkerStatus>>;
\`\`\`

## Progress Feedback Requirements

### 1. Progress Bar for Multi-Worker Operations
\`\`\`rust
use indicatif::{ProgressBar, ProgressStyle};

// In get_fleet_status:
let pb = if !ctx.is_json() && workers.len() > 1 {
    let pb = ProgressBar::new(workers.len() as u64);
    pb.set_style(ProgressStyle::default_bar()
        .template("{spinner:.green} [{bar:40}] {pos}/{len} workers ({eta})")
        .progress_chars("=>-"));
    Some(pb)
} else {
    None
};

// After each worker completes:
if let Some(ref pb) = progress_bar {
    pb.inc(1);
}

// At the end:
if let Some(pb) = progress_bar {
    pb.finish_with_message("Done");
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Per-Worker Status Updates (Verbose Mode)
\`\`\`rust
// When -v/--verbose flag is set:
if ctx.is_verbose() && !ctx.is_json() {
    eprintln!("  {} Checking {}...", StatusIndicator::Pending.display(style), worker.id);
}
// After check:
if ctx.is_verbose() && !ctx.is_json() {
    let icon = if status.healthy { "✓" } else { "✗" };
    eprintln!("  {} {} - {}", icon, worker.id, 
        if status.healthy { "healthy" } else { "issues found" });
}
\`\`\`

## Retry Logic Requirements

### Add Retry Wrapper
\`\`\`rust
async fn with_retry<F, T, E>(
    config: &FleetConfig,
    operation: F,
) -> Result<T, E>
where
    F: Fn() -> impl Future<Output = Result<T, E>>,
    E: std::fmt::Display,
{
    let mut last_error = None;
    for attempt in 0..=config.retry_count {
        match operation().await {
            Ok(result) => return Ok(result),
            Err(e) => {
                if attempt < config.retry_count {
                    tracing::debug!(attempt = %attempt, error = %e, "Retrying after error");
                    tokio::time::sleep(config.retry_delay).await;
                }
                last_error = Some(e);
            }
        }
    }
    Err(last_error.unwrap())
}
\`\`\`

### Use in SSH Operations
\`\`\`rust
let result = with_retry(&config, || async {
    ssh_executor.check_connectivity().await
}).await;
\`\`\`

## CLI Flag Support
\`\`\`bash
# Override timeouts via CLI
rch fleet preflight --timeout 30
rch fleet status --timeout 15 --parallel 5

# Verbose mode
rch fleet preflight -v
rch fleet status --verbose

# Quiet mode (suppress progress)
rch fleet status -q
\`\`\`

## Logging Requirements
\`\`\`rust
tracing::debug!(
    connect_timeout = ?config.ssh_connect_timeout,
    command_timeout = ?config.ssh_command_timeout,
    "Using fleet configuration"
);
tracing::info!(
    workers = %workers.len(),
    parallel = %config.max_concurrent_workers,
    "Starting fleet operation"
);
\`\`\`

 {"id":"bd-ryot","title":"Create unit test infrastructure for TUI components","description":"# Create TUI Unit Test Infrastructure

## Overview
TUI components need specialized testing infrastructure since they render to terminals. We need utilities for testing widget rendering, event handling, and state transitions.

## Components to Create

### 1. TUI Test Harness (rch/src/tui/test_harness.rs)
```rust
#\![cfg(test)]

use ratatui::backend::TestBackend;
use ratatui::Terminal;
use super::state::TuiState;
use super::widgets;

/// Create a test terminal with specified dimensions.
pub fn test_terminal(width: u16, height: u16) -> Terminal<TestBackend> {
    let backend = TestBackend::new(width, height);
    Terminal::new(backend).unwrap()
}

/// Render state and return the buffer as strings.
pub fn render_to_strings(state: &TuiState) -> Vec<String> {
    let mut terminal = test_terminal(80, 24);
    terminal.draw(|f| widgets::render(f, state)).unwrap();
    terminal.backend().buffer()
        .content.chunks(80)
        .map(|row| row.iter().map(|c| c.symbol()).collect())
        .collect()
}

/// Assert that the rendered output contains a substring.
pub fn assert_rendered_contains(state: &TuiState, expected: &str) {
    let output = render_to_strings(state);
    let combined = output.join("\n");
    assert\!(combined.contains(expected), 
        "Expected '{}' in rendered output:\n{}", expected, combined);
}

/// Simulate key press and return new state.
pub fn press_key(state: &mut TuiState, key: KeyCode) {
    // Apply key to state
}
```

### 2. Mock Data Generators (rch/src/tui/test_data.rs)
```rust
#\![cfg(test)]

use super::state::*;

pub fn mock_worker(id: &str, status: WorkerStatus) -> WorkerState {
    WorkerState {
        id: id.to_string(),
        host: format\!("{}.example.com", id),
        status,
        used_slots: 2,
        total_slots: 8,
        circuit: CircuitState::Closed,
        last_probe: Some(Instant::now()),
    }
}

pub fn mock_build(id: &str, status: BuildStatus) -> HistoricalBuild {
    HistoricalBuild {
        id: id.to_string(),
        command: "cargo build".to_string(),
        worker: "css".to_string(),
        status,
        duration: Duration::from_secs(60),
        completed_at: Utc::now(),
    }
}

pub fn mock_state_with_workers(n: usize) -> TuiState {
    let mut state = TuiState::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        state.workers.push(mock_worker(&format\!("worker{}", i), WorkerStatus::Healthy));
    }
    state
}
```
